message:
test(db/cache): extend CacheOp proptest coverage + annotate sigificant-drop-tightening allows

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/cache.rs
- The dual-index cache proptest from 76588c97 treated the
  CacheOp::{Put, Invalidate, Touch, GetByKey, GetById} variants
  as compact tuples. Rewrite them as named-field structs so the
  shrinker output is readable:
    Put { name_idx, project_id, agent_id, email, display_name,
          … }
    Invalidate { name_idx, project_id, with_id: Option<i64> }
    Touch { name_idx, project_id, ts }
    GetByKey { name_idx, project_id }
    GetById { name_idx, project_id }
  Same state space (same arbitrary ranges) but now when the
  shrinker reports a failing CacheOp::Put it actually says which
  field drove the divergence instead of a positional tuple.
- Three `#[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)]` annotations
  added to `ReadCache::{put_agent_scoped, warm_agents_scoped,
  invalidate_agent_scoped}`. Clippy wants the write-lock guard
  held over a shorter span, but we've already documented in
  block-level comments (rank 22 → 23, prevent the
  `put <-> invalidate` race) that the lock MUST cover the entire
  dual-index pair. Allowing the lint here acknowledges the
  deliberate design instead of having contributors try to
  "fix" it in a way that reintroduces br-ctf33.
